name: Diátaxis framework
snippet:
Diátaxis identifies four distinct needs, and four corresponding forms of documentation - tutorials, how-to guides, technical reference and explanation. It ... Read more
Diátaxis identifies four distinct needs, and four corresponding forms of documentation - tutorials, how-to guides, technical reference and explanation. It places them in a systematic relationship, and proposes that documentation should itself be organised around the structures of those needs.

name: What is Diátaxis and should you be using it with your
snippet:
Oct 18, 2023 — The Diátaxis approach to documentation organizes technical documentation into four types — tutorials , how-tos, reference , and explanation . Read more
The Diátaxis approach to documentation organizes technical documentation into four types — tutorials, how-tos, reference, and explanation. In this post, I compare Diátaxis to DITA, Information Mapping, and the Good Docs Project, explaining similarities and differences.
